text: I wouldn't say that this Finals "wasn't real basketball". It was. We saw great defense, and some high level offense to get around that D. We also saw some poor offense, especially down the stretch of games. But players more than ever play to the refs, and the refs are not doing a good enough job of recognizing it. The reffing system needs to be revolutionized. It's definitely time for another rule change to tackle the foul baiting.
label: hot_take
notes: Confident structural claim ("reffing system needs to be revolutionized") with no specific numbers/named calls cited -- general impression dressed as a verdict.

text: I don't think this is actually the answer. The amount of fouls per game is generally pretty constant (it's actually kinda a low average right now), regardless of flopping. The more refs "let them play", the rougher they'll play until a foul is called. I think it's purely an issue of refs calling the wrong fouls.
